keywords: [Multimodal RL, Data Mixture, Vision-Language, RLVR, Multi-Task]

name: modular-large-model-training
description: Train large models efficiently across heterogeneous hardware (GPUs, TPUs, Trainium) using strict encapsulation principles, achieving constant code complexity when adding features across hundreds of modules.
paperTitle: AXLearn: Modular Large Model Training on Heterogeneous Infrastructure
keywords: [Distributed Training, Model Training Framework, Hardware Agnosticism, JAX, Modularity]

name: moe-routing-alignment
description: Prevents MoE router instability during RL training by recording and replaying inference-phase routing distributions back into training. Reduces training-inference routing divergence and KL divergence, enabling stable MoE RL scaling without sacrificing training speed.
paperTitle: Stabilizing MoE RL by Aligning Training and Inference Routers
keywords: [MoE, Routing, Reinforcement Learning, Stability, Training]

name: moe-sparsity-reasoning
description: Determine optimal MoE sparsity by separating memorization and reasoning trade-offs: active FLOPs improve reasoning while total parameters improve memorization, requiring joint optimization
paperTitle: Optimal Sparsity of MoE Language Models for Reasoning
keywords: [mixture-of-experts, sparsity, reasoning, memorization, compute-optimal]

name: molecular-thought-reasoning
description: Improve agent reasoning by designing thought structures that balance deep analysis, self-reflection, and exploratory thinking. Framework discovers that effective long-form reasoning exhibits molecular-like interaction patterns—specific bonds between reasoning components that enable fast entropy convergence. Method synthesizes improved reasoning trajectories using distribution-transfer, improving both model performance and RL training stability.
paperTitle: The Molecular Structure of Thought: Mapping the Topology of Long Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
keywords: [reasoning-structure, chain-of-thought, agent-thinking, reinforcement-learning, reasoning-synthesis]

name: monet-latent-visual-reasoning
description: Enable multimodal LLMs to generate and reason with latent visual embeddings as intermediate thoughts: implement supervised fine-tuning to produce continuous visual representations, then optimize via VLPO to treat embeddings as learnable actions in reinforcement learning.
paperTitle: Monet: Reasoning in Latent Visual Space Beyond Images and Language
keywords: [Latent Visual Reasoning, Chain of Thought, VLM Enhancement, Vision-Language Models]

name: moss-transcribe-diarize-accurate-transcription-wit
description: Research contribution advancing agent and reasoning capabilities through novel approaches to model development, training, and evaluation.
paperTitle: MOSS Transcribe Diarize: Accurate Transcription with Speaker Diarization
keywords: ['agents', 'reasoning', 'systems']

name: motion-stream-real-time-interactive-video
description: Generate videos at 29 FPS with interactive motion control through teacher-student distillation of motion-conditioned video models, using sliding-window causal attention and attention sinks to maintain constant latency for indefinite-length generation.
paperTitle: MotionStream: Real-Time Video Generation with Interactive Motion Controls
keywords: [Video Generation, Real-Time Rendering, Motion Control, Distillation, Attention Mechanisms]

name: mr-align-meta-reasoning-factuality
description: Improve factuality in large reasoning models by analyzing reasoning state transitions and reweighting preference optimization signals, suppressing defective reasoning segments while amplifying patterns that lead to factual outputs.
paperTitle: MR-Align: Meta-Reasoning Informed Factuality Alignment for Large Reasoning Models
keywords: [Reasoning Alignment, Factuality, Meta-Reasoning, Preference Optimization, LLM Reasoning]

name: mr-search-meta-rl-agentic-search
description: Improve agent search through meta-RL: generate multiple episodes sequentially, each building on prior attempts with explicit self-reflection. Use turn-level RLOO advantage estimation to provide dense credit without value models.

keywords: [Meta-RL, Self-Reflection, In-Context Learning, Agent Search, RLOO]

name: msign-stable-rank-restoration
description: Prevent unrecoverable gradient explosions in LLM training by periodically restoring weight matrix stable rank through SVD-based matrix sign operations, eliminating sudden training failures without computational burden.

name: mu-parametrization-moe

name: multi-agent-evolve

name: multi-agent-memory-framework
description: Design multi-agent systems with brain-inspired memory mechanisms that enable efficient information sharing and coordination. Implement hierarchical memory structures (working memory, episodic memory, semantic memory) similar to neuroscience models to improve multi-agent reasoning, planning, and task completion.
paperTitle: BMAM: Brain-inspired Multi-Agent Memory Framework

name: multi-agent-memory-system

paperTitle: MIRIX: Multi-Agent Memory System for LLM-Based Agents

name: multi-agent-tool-policy-opt

name: multi-scale-speculative-decoding
description: Accelerate autoregressive image generation via multi-resolution drafting with spatially-informed verification. Local rejection and resampling enable efficient error correction focusing on spatial neighborhoods, achieving 1.7× speedup over baselines.
paperTitle: Multi-Scale Local Speculative Decoding for Image Generation
keywords: ['Image Generation', 'Speculative Decoding', 'Inference Acceleration']

name: multi-task-grpo-robust
description: Enable balanced multi-task GRPO training via robustness-aware optimization and improvement-aware task reweighting, dynamically adjusting task weights based on both reward and loss trajectory improvement, achieving 6-28% worst-task improvements while maintaining competitive average accuracy.
paperTitle: Multi-Task GRPO: Reliable LLM Reasoning Across Tasks

name: multiagent-communication
description: Enable agents to communicate through shared latent thoughts rather than natural language, recovering both shared and private latent representations with theoretical guarantees for more efficient collaboration.

name: multiagent-process-rewards
description: Train specialized agents in pipelines using dense per-action process rewards from AI coaching. Solves credit assignment in sequential workflows, enabling better generalization and faster convergence than outcome-only training.
paperTitle: Scaling Multiagent Systems with Process Rewards
keywords: [Multiagent Systems, Process Rewards, Credit Assignment, REINFORCE, Pipeline Agents]

name: multimodal-diffusion-alignment
description: Improve text-image alignment in diffusion transformers through Temperature-Adjusted Cross-modal Attention (TACA), addressing token imbalance and timestep-dependent weighting with parameter-efficient LoRA fine-tuning.
paperTitle: Rethinking Cross-Modal Interaction in Multimodal Diffusion Transformers
keywords: [diffusion-models, text-to-image, multimodal, cross-modal-attention, lora-finetuning]

name: multimodal-video-document-embeddings
description: Generate unified embeddings for videos, images, and visual documents enabling semantic similarity, retrieval, and clustering across heterogeneous visual content types.
paperTitle: VLM2Vec-V2: Advancing Multimodal Embedding for Videos, Images, and Visual Documents
keywords: [Multimodal Embeddings, Video Understanding, Document Retrieval, Cross-modal Search, Vision-Language Models]

name: multiverse-parallel-generation
description: Enable native parallel token generation in language models by implementing adaptive task decomposition and merge strategies, achieving 2x speedup with 1.87% performance gains.
paperTitle: Multiverse: Your Language Models Secretly Decide How to Parallelize and Merge Generation
keywords: [parallel generation, MapReduce, non-autoregressive, task decomposition]

name: muon-optimizer-tail-memory-learning
description: Improve LLM training efficiency by selectively applying Muon optimizer to Value-Output attention weights and FFN layers, which function as associative memories. Use when training data exhibits heavy-tailed distributions requiring robust rare-fact learning.
paperTitle: Muon Optimizer: Selective Parameter Optimization for Associative Memory
keywords: [optimizer, muon, transformers, associative-memory, efficiency]

name: muses-designing-composing-generating-nonexistent-f
description: Enhanced language model pre-training methodology improving linguistic competence across languages, strengthening foundational capabilities for multilingual agent systems.
paperTitle: Muses: Designing, Composing, Generating Nonexistent Fantasy 3D Creatures without Training
keywords: ['training']

name: musixqa-visual-music-understanding
description: Teach multimodal LLMs to read and understand sheet music through a synthetic QA dataset with kern+ symbolic notation. Enables models to handle music sheet OCR, symbol recognition, and chord estimation at 8× better performance than GPT-4o.

name: nabla-reasoner
description: Improves LLM reasoning quality at inference time by optimizing token logits using gradient descent, combining reward model signals with KL-regularization. Bridges parametric training-time and non-parametric test-time scaling through token-level optimization.
paperTitle: ∇-Reasoner: LLM Reasoning via Test-Time Gradient Descent in Latent Space
keywords: [Inference Time Scaling, Reasoning, Gradient Descent, Reward Optimization, Latent Space Optimization]

name: nag-diffusion-guidance
description: Apply training-free negative guidance in diffusion models by extrapolating in attention space with L1-based normalization, restoring suppression of unwanted attributes across architectures and modalities.

keywords: [diffusion models, negative guidance, attention, few-step sampling, image generation]

name: native-parallel-reasoner
description: Enable LLMs to develop genuine parallel reasoning without external supervision through progressive self-distilled training. Transform models from sequential reasoning to native parallel cognition with 4.6× speedup—ideal when latency and reasoning quality both matter.
paperTitle: Native Parallel Reasoner: Reasoning in Parallelism via Self-Distilled Reinforcement Learning
keywords: [parallel reasoning, reinforcement learning, teacher-free training, inference speedup, reasoning parallelism]

name: nbdiff-block-diffusion-llm
description: Convert auto-regressive language models to efficient diffusion-based generators through gradual block size increments. NBDiff-7B inherits long-context capabilities from AR predecessors while achieving state-of-the-art parallel generation—ideal when you need efficiency without sacrificing reasoning.
paperTitle: From Next-Token to Next-Block: A Principled Adaptation Path for Diffusion LLMs
keywords: [diffusion language models, block generation, auto-regressive to diffusion, parallel decoding, model adaptation]

name: nemotron-3
description: Build efficient open-source LLMs via hybrid Mamba-Transformer MoE architecture with LatentMoE expert design, multi-token prediction training, FP4 precision, and multi-environment RL post-training—achieving 3.3× higher throughput than equivalently-sized models while maintaining state-of-the-art reasoning, coding, and tool-use capabilities.
paperTitle: NVIDIA Nemotron 3: Efficient and Open Intelligence

name: nemotron-3-nano
description: Efficient agentic reasoning via sparse MoE activating 50% parameters per token. Combines Mamba-Transformer hybrid with 6-of-128 expert routing, three-stage post-training (SFT, verifiable RL, RLHF), and Group Relative Length Control—achieving 3.3× inference throughput of competitors while maintaining 1M token context support and superior reasoning.
paperTitle: Nemotron 3 Nano: MoE Hybrid Mamba-Transformer for Agentic Reasoning
keywords: [mixture-of-experts, mamba-transformer, efficient, agentic, moe]

name: nemotron-cascade-rl
description: Train language models through sequential, domain-wise RL stages (RLHF → Instruction-Following → Math → Code → SWE) without catastrophic forgetting. Exploit policy-dependent training data distribution where previous behaviors persist when reward-relevant. 14B model surpasses DeepSeek-R1-0528 (671B) on LiveCodeBench.

name: nemotron-elastic-efficient-reasoning
description: Deploy multiple reasoning model sizes efficiently by embedding nested submodels within a single parent—use end-to-end trained routers to select submodels at inference, achieving 360× cost reduction vs training families separately.

name: nemotron-flash-latency-optimal
description: Evolutionary architecture search discovering optimal depth-width ratios and operator combinations under deployment latency constraints using augmented scaling laws. Deploy when you need fastest inference per latency target with mixed attention mechanisms.
paperTitle: Nemotron-Flash: Latency-Optimal Hybrid Small Language Models via Augmented Scaling Laws
keywords: [model-architecture, latency-optimization, scaling-laws, hybrid-operators, weight-normalization]

name: nemotron-math-long-context
description: Create 7.5M long-form mathematical solution traces with multi-mode supervision (high/medium/low reasoning depths, with/without Python). Integrate 85K competition problems (AoPS) and 262K community questions (StackExchange). Implement sequential bucketing training achieving 2-3× speedup while maintaining accuracy.
paperTitle: Nemotron-Math: Large-Scale Mathematical Reasoning Dataset with Multi-Mode Supervision and Sequential Bucketing

name: nemotron-nano-hybrid-mamba-transformer
description: Build hybrid Mamba-Transformer models combining efficient Mamba-2 layers with standard attention to achieve 6x higher inference throughput while maintaining reasoning accuracy on long-context tasks.
paperTitle: Nemotron Nano 2: Hybrid Mamba-Transformer Architecture for Efficient Reasoning
keywords: [hybrid-architecture, mamba, transformer, model-compression, reasoning, throughput]

name: nepa-next-embedding-prediction
description: Train vision transformers through autoregressive next-embedding prediction without pixel reconstruction, tokenizers, or contrastive losses. Apply causal masking and stop-gradient on target embeddings. Achieve 83.8% (ViT-B) and 85.3% (ViT-L) ImageNet-1K accuracy with strong transfer to downstream tasks.
paperTitle: NEPA: Next-Embedding Prediction as Self-Supervised Visual Pretraining for Vision Transformers
keywords: [self-supervised-learning, vision-transformers, next-embedding-prediction, autoregressive, visual-pretraining]

name: nestbrowse-info-seeking
description: Scale information-seeking agents via nested architecture separating outer reasoning from inner page exploration. Minimal toolkit (search, visit, click, fill) handles dynamic web interactions, inner loop filters content before context injection, multi-task learning trains outer/inner jointly—enabling 4B models to match larger competitors.
paperTitle: Nested Browser-Use Learning for Agentic Information Seeking

name: neural-thickets-dense-task-experts
description: Discover that large pretrained models have dense neighborhoods of task-specific experts—random weight perturbations improve performance. Use RandOpt: sample perturbations, select top performers, ensemble via voting for multi-task adaptation.
paperTitle: Neural Thickets: Diverse Task Experts Are Dense Around Pretrained Weights
keywords: [Pretraining, Adaptation, Ensembling, Task Specialization, Model Landscape]

name: neuralOS-gui-simulation
description: Simulate GUI behavior by predicting screen frames in response to user inputs. NeuralOS combines hierarchical RNNs for state tracking with diffusion-based rendering, capturing mouse interactions and application state transitions. Trains on synthetic demonstrations plus random exploration; achieves 50-61% human indistinguishability on basic operations while maintaining 18 fps inference on single H100.
paperTitle: NeuralOS: Towards Simulating Operating Systems via Neural Generative Models
keywords: [GUI Simulation, Diffusion Models, RNN State Tracking, Interactive Systems]

name: nextflow-multimodal-generation
description: Build unified decoder-only transformers for multimodal tasks using 6 trillion interleaved text-image tokens with next-scale prediction for visual content—enabling fast 1024x1024 image generation (5 seconds), image editing, and video generation while rivaling specialized diffusion models.
paperTitle: NextFlow: Unified Sequential Modeling Activates Multimodal Understanding and Generation
keywords: [Multimodal Models, Sequential Modeling, Image Generation, Video Generation, Unified Architecture]

name: nextstep-1-autoregressive-images
description: Train a unified autoregressive model to generate images and text by directly handling continuous image tokens with flow matching, eliminating the need for quantization or separate diffusion models.

To address these limitations, we propose OmniTransfer, a unified framework for spatio-temporal video transfer. It leverages multi-view info...

Open-Vision-Reasoner achieves 95.3% on MATH500 and 54.6% on MathVerse by learning visual analogs of backtracking, verification, and subgoal decomposition using rule-based rewards.

name: paper2rebuttal-a-multi-agent-framework-for

While computer graphics and physics-based simulators can easily model such collisions using Newton formulas, modern pretrain-finetune paradigms discard the concept of object rigidity during pixel-level global denoising. Even perfectly correct math...

name: proact-agentic-lookahead
description: Learn to ground LLM agent planning in real environment dynamics using Monte-Carlo Tree Search exploration combined with lightweight Monte-Carlo critics, reducing hallucination-driven planning failures in interactive tasks.
paperTitle: ProAct: Agentic Lookahead in Interactive Environments
keywords: [Agentic Planning, MCTS, World Models, LLM Agents, Simulation Drift]

name: proactive-self-refinement
description: Enable models to refine outputs dynamically during generation based on internal signals, reducing token consumption by 41.6% while improving accuracy by 8.2%.
paperTitle: A Stitch in Time: Proactive Self-Refinement for Language Models
keywords: [self-refinement, dynamic-revision, in-generation-improvement, token-efficiency]

name: profuse-3d-semantic-understanding
description: Apply semantic understanding to 3D Gaussian Splatting scenes through dense correspondence-guided pre-registration without render-supervised fine-tuning. Achieve semantic understanding in ~5 minutes using cross-view clustering and direct language feature fusion.
paperTitle: ProFuse: Efficient Cross-View Context Fusion for Open-Vocabulary 3D Gaussian Splatting
keywords: ['3D Vision', 'Semantic Understanding', 'Gaussian Splatting']

name: progressive-residual-warmup
description: Improves LLM convergence and downstream task performance by introducing time-dependent scaling to residual connections, enabling shallow layers to learn first before deeper layers activate. Apply during model pretraining to achieve 0.4-4.86 perplexity reduction.
paperTitle: Progressive Residual Warmup for Language Model Pretraining
keywords: [Pretraining, Optimization, Residual Connections, Layer-wise Warmup, Gradient Stability]

name: prompt-orchestration-markup
description: Use component-based markup with CSS-like styling to structure complex prompts, integrate diverse data types, and separate content from formatting for maintainable, version-control-friendly LLM applications.
paperTitle: Prompt Orchestration Markup Language (POML)
keywords: [prompt-engineering, markup-language, structured-prompts, templating, developer-tools]

name: promptquine-evolving-in-context-prompts
description: Automatically discovers optimal in-context learning prompts through evolutionary token pruning that removes redundant demonstrations to create effective 'gibberish' prompts. Matches state-of-the-art optimization with low-data regimes. Use for automated prompt discovery without manual tuning or human expertise.
paperTitle: Evolving Prompts In-Context: An Open-ended, Self-replicating Perspective
keywords: [PromptOptimization, EvolutionarySearch, InContextLearning, AutomatedPrompting]

name: prophet-diffusion-lm
description: Detect when diffusion language models converge on correct answers before completing refinement steps using confidence gap monitoring, achieving 3.4x decoding speedup
paperTitle: Prophet Early Answer Convergence in Diffusion Language Models
keywords: [diffusion-lm, early-stopping, confidence-estimation, acceleration, decoding]

name: prorl-reasoning-expansion
description: Train LLMs to discover novel reasoning strategies beyond base model capabilities using prolonged RL with KL control, reference policy resets, and diverse task suites.
paperTitle: ProRL: Prolonged Reinforcement Learning Expands Reasoning Boundaries in Large Language Models
keywords: [Reinforcement Learning, Reasoning, Language Models, GRPO, Test-Time Computation]

name: psa-pyramid-sparse-attention
description: Replaces binary keep/drop masks with multi-level pooled key-value representations, allowing queries to access larger receptive fields under same compute budget through hierarchical aggregation without discarding information.
paperTitle: PSA: Pyramid Sparse Attention for Video Understanding and Generation
keywords: [sparse-attention, video-understanding, video-generation, hierarchical-pooling, efficient-transformers]

name: puzzle-curriculum-grpo
description: Post-train vision-language models using automatically-verifiable puzzle environments (Jigsaw, Rotation, PatchFit) with graded rewards. Implement exploration-aware curriculum combining difficulty weighting with solution-space diversity metrics. Track reasoning-answer consistency to prevent divergence during training.
paperTitle: Puzzle Curriculum: Vision Language Model Post-training via Self-Supervised Puzzle Tasks and Exploration-Aware Curriculum
keywords: [vision-language, reinforcement-learning, curriculum-learning, self-supervised, puzzle-tasks]

name: pvchat-personalized-video-one-shot
description: Enable identity-aware video question answering with one-shot learning using Mixture-of-Heads enhanced ViLLM. Learns subject-specific features from single video through synthetic augmentation and progressive image-to-video training, enabling recognition of individuals in medical, smart home, and entertainment contexts.
paperTitle: PVChat: Personalized Video Chat with One-Shot Learning
keywords: [One-Shot Learning, Video Understanding, Personalization, Mixture-of-Heads, Identity-Aware QA]

name: pyramidal-wan-video-efficiency
description: Convert pretrained video diffusion models into pyramidal architectures via low-cost finetuning while preserving output quality. Explore step distillation for enhanced efficiency, enabling deployment of efficient inference without training from scratch.
paperTitle: PyramidalWan: On Making Pretrained Video Model Pyramidal for Efficient Inference
keywords: ['Video Processing', 'Model Efficiency', 'Pyramidal Architectures']

name: pyvision-agentic-vision-dynamic-tools
description: Enable multimodal language models to autonomously generate and execute Python-based tools during visual reasoning, boosting performance on vision benchmarks by up to 31% through interactive problem-solving without relying on predefined tool sets.
paperTitle: PyVision: Agentic Vision with Dynamic Tooling
keywords: [Agentic Vision, Tool Generation, Visual Reasoning, Code Execution, Dynamic Programming]

name: q-tuning-joint-pruning-efficient-training
description: Dramatically reduce training data requirements (to 12.5% of original) while improving model performance using joint sample and token pruning guided by Error-Uncertainty plane diagnostics. Asymmetric pruning preserves calibration signals while removing redundant tokens from misconception examples.
paperTitle: Winning the Pruning Gamble: A Unified Approach to Joint Sample and Token Pruning for Efficient Supervised Fine-Tuning
keywords: [data-pruning, token-pruning, training-efficiency, data-selection, fine-tuning]

name: qerl-quantization-rl
description: Combine NFVP4 quantization with LoRA to accelerate RL rollout phases while using quantization noise as implicit exploration bonus. Achieve 1.5x speedup and better strategy discovery through noise-enhanced policy entropy.
paperTitle: QeRL: Beyond Efficiency -- Quantization-enhanced Reinforcement Learning for LLMs
keywords: [quantization, reinforcement-learning, llm-training, model-compression, exploration]

name: quantile-advantage-entropy-safe-reasoning
description: Stabilize LLM reasoning training by replacing mean-based advantage baselines with K-quantile baselines, preventing both entropy collapse and explosion while improving performance on mathematical benchmarks through response-level gating and asymmetric sample weighting.
paperTitle: Quantile Advantage Estimation: Stabilizing RLVR for LLM Reasoning
keywords: [RLVR, LLM reasoning, quantile baselines, entropy stability, advantage estimation, policy optimization, mathematical reasoning, training stability]

name: quantized-evolution-strategies
description: Fine-tune quantized LLMs directly in low-precision discrete parameter space using evolution strategies with accumulated error feedback. Overcome gradient stagnation in quantized models by accumulating fractional updates using Delta-Sigma modulation, achieving significant improvements in INT4 quantized models without full-precision gradients.
paperTitle: Quantized Evolution Strategies: High-precision Fine-tuning of Quantized LLMs
keywords: [Quantization, Fine-Tuning, Evolution Strategies, Gradient Stagnation, Memory Efficiency]

name: quco-rag
description: Replace unreliable model-internal confidence signals with objective corpus statistics to decide when RAG retrieval is necessary. Pre-evaluates entity rarity in training data and verifies entity co-occurrence at runtime, triggering retrieval only when hallucination risk is high—improving reliability without per-model tuning.
paperTitle: QuCo-RAG: Quantifying Uncertainty for Dynamic RAG
keywords: [rag, retrieval, uncertainty, hallucination, calibration]

name: query-bandits-hallucination
description: QueryBandits adaptively learns per-query rewriting strategies to reduce LLM hallucinations, achieving 87.5% improvement without model retraining.
paperTitle: No One Size Fits All: QueryBandits for Hallucination Mitigation
keywords: [Hallucination Mitigation, Contextual Bandits, Query Rewriting, LLM Optimization, Thompson Sampling]

name: qwen3-embedding
description: Leverages Qwen3 foundation models for text embedding and reranking via multi-stage training combining weakly-supervised pre-training on 150M synthetic pairs with supervised fine-tuning.
paperTitle: Qwen3 Embedding: Advancing Text Embedding and Reranking Through Foundation Models
keywords: [text-embeddings, reranking, foundation-models, multilingual, retrieval]

name: qwen3-vl-multimodal
description: State-of-the-art multimodal model advancing vision-language understanding and generation capabilities through improved visual encoders, dense token representations, and unified reasoning over images and text.
paperTitle: Qwen3-VL: Advanced Vision-Language Understanding and Generation
keywords: [vision-language, multimodal-models, image-understanding, dense-captioning, visual-reasoning]

name: qwenlong-long-context-reasoning
description: Systematically post-train models for long-context reasoning through multi-hop data synthesis, stabilized RL with adaptive entropy control, and memory-augmented architecture supporting 4M+ token sequences. Achieves performance comparable to GPT-5 and Gemini-2.5-Pro on long-context benchmarks.
paperTitle: QwenLong-L1.5: Post-training for Long-Context Reasoning and Memory-Augmented Generation
keywords: [long-context, reasoning, memory-augmented, reinforcement-learning, multi-hop-grounding]

name: r-horizon-long-horizon-reasoning
description: Construct multi-step reasoning benchmarks with interdependent problems to evaluate and improve long-horizon reasoning in large reasoning models. Enables evaluation of reasoning depth and breadth beyond single-step tasks.
paperTitle: R-Horizon: How Far Can Your Large Reasoning Model Really Go in Breadth and Depth?
keywords: [reasoning, benchmark, long-horizon, query-composition, large-reasoning-models]

name: r-wom-world-model
description: Ground LLM world models with retrieved current knowledge from tutorials and documentation. Reduce hallucination in environment prediction and improve long-horizon planning by 16-23% on web agent benchmarks.
paperTitle: R-WoM: Retrieval-augmented World Model For Computer-use Agents
keywords: [world-model, retrieval-augmentation, agent-planning, environment-simulation, computer-use]

name: r-zero-self-evolving-reasoning
description: Co-evolutionary framework where Challenger generates tasks and Solver solves them. Models evolve autonomously from scratch without human annotations. Improves math reasoning +6.49pts and general reasoning +7.54pts.
paperTitle: R-Zero - Self-Evolving Reasoning LLM from Zero Data
keywords: [self-play, co-evolution, reasoning, data-generation, bootstrapping]

name: radial-attention-video
description: Accelerate video diffusion models using sparse radial attention that exploits energy decay patterns. Achieves 3.7× speedup on long videos while maintaining quality through O(n log n) complexity instead of O(n²).
paperTitle: Radial Attention: O(nlog n) Sparse Attention with Energy Decay for Long Video Generation
keywords: [Sparse Attention, Video Generation, Long Sequence, Computational Efficiency, Diffusion Models]

name: ral-reinforced-attention-learning
description: Optimize where multimodal models attend by treating attention weights as a learnable policy, using policy gradients with advantage weighting to improve visual grounding and perception without changing model architecture.
paperTitle: Reinforced Attention Learning
keywords: [Multimodal Learning, Attention Optimization, Policy Gradient, Perception, Vision-Language Models]

name: ramp-adaptive-quantization
description: Learn optimal per-layer bit-width assignments for LLM quantization via RL, generalizing across models without retraining. Achieves superior compression under fixed bit budgets.
paperTitle: RAMP: Reinforcement Adaptive Mixed Precision Quantization for On-Device LLM Inference
keywords: [Quantization, Mixed Precision, On-Device Inference, Reinforcement Learning]

name: rank-one-safety-alignment
description: Apply rank-one weight modifications to amplify model safety via residual stream steering, requiring no fine-tuning and preserving utility on standard benchmarks
paperTitle: Rank-One Safety Injection for Lightweight Alignment
keywords: [safety-alignment, weight-steering, activation-space, lightweight, fine-tuning-free]

name: re-align-reasoning-image-generation
description: Bridge image understanding-generation gap via In-Context Chain-of-Thought reasoning and RL training with surrogate rewards. Improve faithful execution of mixed image-text prompts in generation and editing tasks.
paperTitle: Re-Align: Structured Reasoning-guided Alignment for In-Context Image Generation and Editing
keywords: ['Image Generation', 'Vision-Language Models', 'Alignment']

name: re-bottleneck-latent-restructuring
description: Restructure latent representations in pretrained audio autoencoders without full retraining. Apply three variants—ordered, semantic, and equivariant—to enforce structure like channel ordering, semantic alignment, or filter correspondence. Achieves 20-60% semantic gains in under 48 GPU hours versus 14.5K hours for full retraining.
paperTitle: Re-Bottleneck: Latent Re-Structuring for Neural Audio Autoencoders
keywords: [Audio Autoencoders, Latent Space, Post-hoc Modification, Semantic Alignment]

name: re-trac-trajectory-compression
description: Compress search trajectories into structured states capturing partial answers, evidence, and uncertainties. Recursive execution leverages compressed states to avoid redundant exploration, improving resource efficiency by 50%.
paperTitle: RE-TRAC: REcursive TRAjectory Compression for Deep Search Agents
keywords: [Search Agents, Trajectory Compression, Recursive Planning, Evidence Tracking]

name: realmem-memory-benchmark
description: Implements RealMem from arXiv:2601.06966
paperTitle: RealMem: Benchmarking LLMs in Real-World Memory-Driven Interaction
keywords: ["Realmem", "Benchmarking Llms In Real", "World Memory", "Driven Interaction"]

name: reason-rank-passage-ranking
description: Enhances passage ranking through reasoning capabilities via synthesized training data and multi-stage training combining supervised fine-tuning with reinforcement learning for improved ranking accuracy.
paperTitle: ReasonRank - Passage Ranking with Reasoning
keywords: [passage-ranking, reasoning, information-retrieval, reranking, synthetic-training-data]

name: reasongen-r1-cot-image-generation
description: Enable image generators to reason explicitly through text before creating images using supervised fine-tuning and reinforcement learning optimization.
paperTitle: ReasonGen-R1: CoT for Autoregressive Image generation models through SFT and RL
keywords: [CoT, Image Generation, SFT, RL, Reasoning]

name: reasoning-as-compression
description: Optimize chain-of-thought reasoning under computational budgets using information-theoretic compression principles, improving reasoning efficiency without accuracy loss.
paperTitle: Reasoning as Compression: Unifying Budget Forcing via the Conditional Information Bottleneck
keywords: [Information Theory, Chain-of-Thought, Budget Optimization, Efficient Reasoning]

name: reasoning-core-synthetic-data
description: Reasoning Core procedurally generates verifiable symbolic reasoning datasets across formal domains (planning, logic, parsing), with external solvers and curriculum control.
paperTitle: Reasoning Core: A Scalable Procedural Data Generation Suite for Symbolic Pre/Post-Training
keywords: [Synthetic Data, Symbolic Reasoning, Curriculum Learning, Data Generation, Verification]

name: reasoning-gym-verifiable-rewards
description: Create infinite training environments for reasoning with automatic verification using procedural generation and domain-specific evaluators.
paperTitle: REASONING GYM: Reasoning Environments for Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards
keywords: [Reinforcement Learning, Verifiable Rewards, Reasoning, Procedural Generation]

name: reasoning-models-generate-societies-of-thought
description: Large language models have achieved remarkable capabilities across domains, yet mechanisms underlying sophisticated reasoning remain elusive. Recent reasoning models outperform comparable instruction-tuned models on complex cognitive tasks, attributed to extended computation through longer chains of thought. Here we show that enhanced reasoning emerges not from extended computation alone, but from simulating multi-agent-like interactions -- a society of thought -- which enables diversification a...
paperTitle: Reasoning Models Generate Societies of Thought
keywords: [Reasoning]

name: reasoning-palette
description: Overcome token-level randomness limitations in RL by shifting exploration to latent reasoning strategies. Train a VAE encoding diverse reasoning patterns, sample latents during RL, decode to prefix embeddings steering internal reasoning—enabling structured exploration across math, coding, and QA with interpretable, controllable behavior.
paperTitle: Reasoning Palette: Modulating Reasoning via Latent Contextualization

name: reasoning-path-confidence
description: Reduce LLM sampling costs by 50% while maintaining reasoning performance through Reasoning Path Confidence (RPC), which combines perplexity-guided pruning with self-consistency sampling.
paperTitle: A Theoretical Study on Bridging Internal Probability and Self-Consistency for LLM Reasoning
keywords: [LLM reasoning, test-time scaling, self-consistency, perplexity, probabilistic sampling]

name: reasoning-vectors-cot-task-arithmetic
description: Extract and transfer reasoning capabilities between language models using task vectors derived from supervised fine-tuning and reinforcement learning weight differences. Apply reasoning vectors via simple arithmetic to enhance any compatible instruction-tuned model without retraining.
paperTitle: Reasoning Vectors: Transferring Chain-of-Thought Capabilities via Task Arithmetic
keywords: [task vectors, chain-of-thought, reasoning transfer, model merging, weight arithmetic, fine-tuning, reinforcement learning, capability composition]

name: reasoning-via-sampling
description: Improves base model reasoning through iterative sampling without training or fine-tuning. Uses MCMC-inspired sampling to extract latent reasoning from pretrained models, achieving RL-comparable gains on math, coding, and QA tasks while preserving diversity.
paperTitle: Reasoning with Sampling: Your Base Model is Smarter Than You Think
keywords: [Sampling, MCMC, Inference, Reasoning, Base Models]

name: rebalance-efficient-reasoning
description: Diagnose and correct reasoning inefficiencies (overthinking and underthinking) in large reasoning models using confidence-based steering vectors, without retraining. Enables optimal reasoning budgets across model scales.
paperTitle: ReBalance: Efficient Reasoning with Balanced Thinking
keywords: [Reasoning Models, Computational Efficiency, Confidence-Guided Steering, Training-Free Optimization]

name: rebuttalagent-strategic-persuasion-in-academic-reb
description: Implement techniques from RebuttalAgent: Strategic Persuasion in Academic Rebuttal via Theory of Mind. Although artificial intelligence (AI) has become deeply integrated into various stages of the research workflow and achieved remarkable advancements, academic rebuttal remains a significant and underexplored challenge
paperTitle: RebuttalAgent: Strategic Persuasion in Academic Rebuttal via Theory of Mind
keywords: ["agent"]

name: recall-hierarchical-merging
description: Prevents catastrophic forgetting in continual learning by merging models using layer-wise hidden representations as similarity proxies. Shallow layers preserve domain-general features while deep layers enable task-specific adaptation, enabling seamless multi-domain integration without task labels or historical data.
paperTitle: RECALL: Catastrophic-forgetting Alleviation via Hierarchical Model Merging
keywords: [Continual Learning, Model Merging, Catastrophic Forgetting, Representations]

name: recode-unified-planning
description: Unifies planning and action by treating plans as abstract placeholder functions recursively decomposed to primitive actions. Enables agents to dynamically adjust abstraction levels per task without rigid hierarchies. Improves inference performance and training efficiency through automatic multi-level data generation.
paperTitle: ReCode: Unify Plan and Action for Universal Granularity Control
keywords: [Planning, Agent, Hierarchical Control, Code Generation, Abstraction]

name: recon-act-self-evolving-browser-agents
description: Build self-evolving multi-agent browser systems that combine web reconnaissance with dynamic tool generation and execution. Enables autonomous agents to analyze failed trajectories, generate specialized tools on-the-fly, and adapt to novel web environments without pre-defined action sets, achieving 36.48% success on VisualWebArena.
paperTitle: Recon-Act: A Self-Evolving Multi-Agent Browser-Use System via Web Reconnaissance, Tool Generation, and Task Execution
keywords: [multi-agent systems, web automation, browser agents, tool generation, self-evolution, reconnaissance, task execution, autonomous web browsing]

name: rectified-sparse-attention
description: Enable efficient long-sequence generation by combining block-sparse attention with periodic dense rectification to bound error accumulation.
paperTitle: Rectified Sparse Attention: Efficient Long-Sequence Generation with Error Correction
keywords: [sparse-attention, efficient-inference, long-sequences, error-rectification]

name: recurrence-memory-reasoning-depth
description: Extend neural network reasoning capabilities through recurrence (repeated computation cycles), external memory (intermediate state storage), and test-time compute scaling for multi-step reasoning.
paperTitle: Extending Reasoning Depth with Recurrence, Memory and Test-Time Compute
keywords: [reasoning-depth, recurrent-computation, external-memory, test-time-scaling, sequential-reasoning]

name: recurrent-depth-vla
description: Enable test-time compute scaling in vision-language-action models via weight-tied recurrent inference within latent space, with adaptive stopping based on action divergence.
paperTitle: Recurrent-Depth VLA: Implicit Test-Time Compute Scaling of VLA Models
keywords: [Vision-Language-Action Models, Latent Reasoning, Adaptive Computation, Robotics, Weight-Tied Recurrence]

name: recursive-language-models
description: Process prompts exceeding model context windows by recursively decomposing long inputs into manageable chunks and calling the model recursively on snippets—enabling inference on contexts 100x longer than native window while maintaining quality and improving over vanilla baseline approaches.
paperTitle: Recursive Language Models
keywords: [Long Context, Inference Scaling, Recursive Decomposition, LLM Architecture, Context Windows]

name: recursive-think-answer-process
description: Recursive Think-Answer Process enables models to iteratively refine reasoning and answers during inference, reducing self-correction errors and improving accuracy without retraining.
paperTitle: Recursive Think-Answer Process for LLMs and VLMs
keywords: [Inference-Time Optimization, Iterative Reasoning, Self-Refinement, Chain-of-Thought, VLM]

name: redbench-a-universal-dataset-for-comprehensive-red
description: Comprehensive evaluation dataset for systematic vulnerability testing of language models, enabling identification and mitigation of failure modes before agent deployment.
paperTitle: RedBench: A Universal Dataset for Comprehensive Red Teaming of Large Language Models
keywords: ['llm']

name: redsearcher-long-horizon-agents
description: Train language models for multi-step information-seeking using dual-constrained task synthesis and cost-efficient staged learning. Generate complex queries by controlling topological complexity and information dispersion, then train atomic reasoning skills before introducing environment interaction. Achieve state-of-the-art on long-horizon search benchmarks with 30B parameter models.
paperTitle: REDSearcher: Scalable Framework for Long-Horizon Search Agents
keywords: [Information Seeking, Long-Horizon Planning, Reinforcement Learning, Search Agents, Tool Use]

name: reflexi-coder
description: Trains LLMs to autonomously debug and improve code through structured RL-optimized reflection cycles. Internalizes debugging process into model weights rather than relying on external oracles or expensive iterative prompting.
paperTitle: ReflexiCoder: Teaching LLMs to Self-Reflect on Generated Code via RL
keywords: [Code Generation, Self-Refinement, Reinforcement Learning, Iterative Improvement, GRPO]

name: refusal-falls-off-cliff-safety-reasoning
description: Identify and patch critical safety vulnerabilities in large reasoning models. Via linear probing and causal intervention, locate specific attention heads responsible for alignment degradation at final tokens. Recover safety via 'Cliff-as-a-Judge' data curation targeting examples exhibiting largest refusal decline, achieving comparable improvements using only 1.7% of vanilla safety training data.
paperTitle: Refusal Falls off a Cliff: How Safety Alignment Fails in Reasoning
keywords: [safety alignment, reasoning models, mechanistic interpretability, refusal cliff, attention analysis]

name: regft-reference-guided-finetuning
description: ReGFT pre-trains models on hybrid reference-augmented trajectories before RL, enabling them to solve harder problems and accelerate convergence.
paperTitle: Learn Hard Problems During RL with Reference Guided Fine-tuning
keywords: [Reinforcement Learning, Fine-Tuning, Mathematical Reasoning, Curriculum Learning, Reference Solutions]

name: rehy-at-video-diffusion-attention
description: Merge softmax and linear attention for video diffusion models using chunk-wise recurrent reformulation with constant memory usage. Enable efficient distillation from existing softmax models, reducing training cost two orders of magnitude to ~160 GPU hours.
paperTitle: ReHyAt: Recurrent Hybrid Attention for Video Diffusion Transformers
keywords: ['Video Generation', 'Attention Mechanisms', 'Efficient Inference']

name: reinforce-ada-adaptive-sampling-rl-llms
description: Recover learning signals in RL for LLM reasoning by dynamically allocating sampling budget based on prompt difficulty. Use log-objective weighting (1/p for pass rate p) to prioritize challenging examples, achieving 2x convergence speedup versus uniform sampling while maintaining identical compute budgets across math, coding, and general benchmarks.
paperTitle: Reinforce-Ada: An Adaptive Sampling Framework under Non-linear RL Objectives
keywords: [adaptive sampling, non-linear RL, gradient estimation, RL for reasoning, signal recovery]

name: reinforcement-distillation-negative-signals
description: Extract maximum value from limited reasoning traces by leveraging both successful and failed examples through REINFORCE-style distillation.
paperTitle: Harnessing Negative Signals: Reinforcement Distillation from Teacher Data for LLM Reasoning
keywords: [Distillation, Reinforcement Learning, Negative Signals, Chain-of-Thought]

name: reinforcement-learning-vision-models
description: Train multimodal LLMs with RL (PIVOT) instead of SFT to produce stronger, precisely-localized visual representations in vision encoders using <1% of standard pretraining cost.
paperTitle: RL makes MLLMs see better than SFT
keywords: [multimodal LLM, vision optimization, reinforcement learning, MLLM training, visual representations]

name: reinforcement-mid-training
description: Add an intermediate RL stage between pretraining and post-training using dynamic token budgeting, curriculum sampling, and dual training. Trigger: reduce reasoning steps while maintaining or improving performance in post-training.
paperTitle: Reinforcement Mid-Training: Intermediate Optimization Between Pretraining and Post-Training
keywords: [intermediate-training, reinforcement-learning, token-optimization, curriculum-learning, reasoning]

name: reinforcement-pretraining
description: Apply reinforcement learning to pre-training by framing next-token prediction as a reasoning task with verifiable rewards, achieving superior scaling compared to standard language modeling.

keywords: [reinforcement-learning, pretraining, scaling-laws, next-token-prediction, reasoning]

name: relay-gen-model-switching
description: Reduce inference cost by dynamically switching from large to small LLMs during reasoning generation. Large model handles demanding reasoning phases; small model completes consolidation and answer stages triggered by discourse cues. Achieves 2.2× speedup with minimal accuracy loss.
paperTitle: RelayGen: Intra-Generation Model Switching for Efficient Reasoning
keywords: [Model Composition, Inference Efficiency, Reasoning Decomposition, Speculative Decoding, Cost-Quality Tradeoff]

name: relayllm-efficient-reasoning-collaboration
description: Enable small language models to dynamically invoke larger models at critical reasoning tokens rather than offloading entire queries. RelayLLM achieves 49.52% accuracy across benchmarks while invoking the large model for only 1.07% of tokens—98.2% cost reduction compared to non-collaborative approaches.
paperTitle: RelayLLM: Efficient Reasoning via Collaborative Decoding
keywords: [Efficient Inference, Multi-Model Collaboration, Token-Level Routing, Reasoning Optimization]

name: reliable-rl-evaluation-contamination-detection
description: Detect and eliminate data contamination that invalidates RL benchmarks by measuring benchmark reconstruction ability. Implement clean evaluation datasets to distinguish genuine reasoning improvements from memorization. Use when validating RL training results or ensuring benchmark integrity for mathematical reasoning.
paperTitle: Reasoning or Memorization? Unreliable Results of Reinforcement Learning Due to Data Contamination
keywords: [Data Contamination, Reinforcement Learning, Model Evaluation, Memorization Detection, Benchmark Integrity]

name: relic-video-world-model
description: Compressed historical latents with camera poses in KV cache (4× compression), extended teacher training (20-second sequences), and replayed back-propagation (block-wise differentiation) enabling real-time interactive video generation with long-range spatial consistency.
paperTitle: RELIC: Interactive Video World Models with Long-Horizon Memory
keywords: [world-models, video-generation, spatial-memory, robotics, long-horizon-planning]

name: remit-rl-guided-mid-training
description: Improve LLM reasoning by reweighting pre-training data during mid-training based on discrepancies between RL-tuned and base models, boosting reasoning performance without external teachers or extra data.
paperTitle: ReMiT: RL-Guided Mid-Training for Iterative LLM Evolution
keywords: [Mid-Training, Token Reweighting, RL Feedback, Curriculum Learning, Reasoning]

name: remix-lora-routing
description: Learn to route requests across multiple LoRA adapters using RL-based router training with constant routing weights. Prevents weight collapse and ensures balanced contribution from all selected adapters during inference.
paperTitle: ReMix: Reinforcement routing for mixtures of LoRAs in LLM finetuning
keywords: [LoRA, Routing, Mixture-of-Experts, RLVR, Reinforcement Learning]

name: repair-robust-editing-llms
description: Perform robust, low-cost model updates via REPAIR: closed-loop feedback, dynamic memory management, and frequent knowledge fusion. Preserve non-target knowledge while achieving 10-30% improvements in editing accuracy. Enable sequential edits without catastrophic forgetting through strong locality guards and stable integration mechanisms.
paperTitle: REPAIR: Robust Editing via Progressive Adaptive Intervention and Reintegration
keywords: [model editing, knowledge correction, lifelong learning, knowledge preservation, sequential edits]

name: repo-context-repositioning
description: Enable language models to dynamically assign continuous position values to tokens based on contextual relevance instead of fixed linear positions. Implement learnable SwiGLU module extracting position information, integrate with RoPE for end-to-end optimization. Improves performance on noisy contexts and long-sequence tasks.
paperTitle: RePo: Dynamic Context Re-positioning for Language Models via Learnable Position Assignment
keywords: [position-encoding, context-reorganization, language-models, learned-positioning, cognitive-load]

name: representation-shift-token-compression
description: Training-free token importance metric using representation shifts in MLP layers, enabling efficient compression compatible with fused kernels.
paperTitle: Representation Shift - Unified Token Importance with FlashAttention
keywords: [token-compression, attention-pruning, inference-optimization, flashattention]

name: repro-process-reward
description: Dual-scoring mechanism (Magnitude and Stability scores) enabling process-level rewards that penalize inefficient reasoning patterns like overthinking and backtracking without trained reward models. Improve reasoning efficiency in RL without additional supervision.
paperTitle: Rectifying LLM Thought from Lens of Optimization: Process-Level Rewards
keywords: [reinforcement-learning, reasoning-optimization, process-rewards, llm-alignment, chain-of-thought]

name: rerec-agent-development-release-engineering
description: Apply software release engineering practices to self-improving LLM agents to achieve stable evolution. AgentDevel uses implementation-blind critique, executable diagnosis, and flip-centered gating to prevent regression while enabling auditable improvement trajectories.
paperTitle: AgentDevel: Reframing Self-Evolving LLM Agents as Release Engineering
keywords: [LLM Agents, Self-Improvement, Release Engineering, Quality Assurance]

name: resa-transparent-reasoning
description: Extract and transfer reasoning abilities using sparse autoencoders (SAE-Tuning) on CoT-free data, achieving RL-equivalent performance at 2000x lower cost and 450x faster training.
paperTitle: Resa: Transparent Reasoning Models via SAEs
keywords: [sparse autoencoders, reasoning transfer, interpretability, efficient training, reasoning tuning]

name: rest-stress-testing-reasoning
description: Evaluate large reasoning models under stress by asking multiple problems simultaneously, revealing true multi-tasking capacity and context management. Use to identify robustness gaps invisible in single-problem evaluations and discriminate model capabilities beyond traditional benchmarks.
paperTitle: REST: Stress Testing Large Reasoning Models by Asking Multiple Problems at Once
keywords: [Model Evaluation, Stress Testing, Reasoning Models, Benchmark Design, Multi-Problem Prompts]

name: restok-learning-hierarchical-residuals-in-1d-visua
description: Generative approach for creating complex dynamic scenes and content, supporting agent capabilities in understanding and reasoning about multi-agent environments.
paperTitle: ResTok: Learning Hierarchical Residuals in 1D Visual Tokenizers for Autoregressive Image Generation
keywords: ['vision', 'generation']

name: rethinking-thinking-tokens-improvement-operators
description: Enable longer reasoning within bounded context through iterative refinement: generate solution → verify → compress → refine. Achieves +11% on AIME with lower per-call latency than naive chain-of-thought.
paperTitle: Rethinking Thinking Tokens: LLMs as Improvement Operators
keywords: [reasoning, thinking-tokens, inference-optimization, verifiable-rewards]

name: retroagent-dual-intrinsic-feedback
description: Train LLM agents to evolve by generating dual intrinsic feedback signals—numerical rewards for capability progress and language lessons for future reuse. Combines hindsight reflection with memory-augmented RL using SimUtil-UCB retrieval.
paperTitle: RetroAgent: From Solving to Evolving via Retrospective Dual Intrinsic Feedback
keywords: [RL, Agent Learning, Self-Reflection, Intrinsic Feedback, Memory]

name: retrofitted-recurrence-deeper-thinking-lms
description: Convert pretrained non-recurrent language models into depth-recurrent variants through a curriculum of increasing recurrence—decoupling training compute from inference compute and improving performance at given inference budgets on reasoning tasks.
paperTitle: Teaching Pretrained LMs to Think Deeper with Retrofitted Recurrence
keywords: [Model Architecture, Recurrent Networks, Inference Optimization, Depth Curriculum, Language Models]

name: revisual-multimodal-reasoning
description: Develop sophisticated multimodal reasoning through text-centric cold-start initialization, prioritized advantage distillation, and staged RL refinement.
paperTitle: ReVisual-R1: Multimodal Reasoning with Cold-Start and Staged RL
keywords: [multimodal-reasoning, reinforcement-learning, curriculum-learning, vision-language]

name: reward-guided-multimodal-decoding
description: Control MLLM output characteristics at inference time using separate reward models for precision and recall, enabling dynamic trade-offs without retraining.
paperTitle: Controlling Multimodal LLMs via Reward-guided Decoding
keywords: [multimodal-llm, reward-modeling, decoding-control, object-grounding, hallucination-reduction]

name: rewardmap-sparse-rewards-visual-reasoning
description: Improve multimodal LLMs on fine-grained visual reasoning tasks (e.g., reading transit maps) by decomposing training into stages: basic perception (VQA) -> simple reasoning -> complex spatial reasoning. Incorporates 'detail rewards' for intermediate visual understanding, bootstrapping models from simple to complex tasks while addressing sparse reward challenges.
paperTitle: RewardMap: Tackling Sparse Rewards in Fine-grained Visual Reasoning via Multi-Stage Reinforcement Learning
keywords: [reward-design, visual-reasoning, sparse-rewards, multi-stage-RL, spatial-reasoning]

name: rgs-slam-robust-gaussian-splatting-slam-with-one-s
description: Advanced computer vision technique for robust spatial understanding in complex scenes, supporting agent navigation and environmental reasoning capabilities.
paperTitle: RGS-SLAM: Robust Gaussian Splatting SLAM with One-Shot Dense Initialization
keywords: ['agents', 'reasoning', 'systems']

name: rise-robot
description: Enable robot policies to self-improve through imagination using learned dynamics and value models without physical trial-and-error. Compositional world model separates concerns enabling 35-45% performance gains on contact-rich manipulation.
paperTitle: RISE: Self-Improving Robot Policy with Compositional World Model
keywords: [Robot Learning, World Models, Reinforcement Learning, Imagination, Policy Optimization, Compositional]

name: rl-anything-dynamic
description: Enable simultaneous optimization of environment difficulty, policy, and reward model. System uses reward model evaluations to guide environment adaptation, creating positive feedback loop for scalable agent improvement.
paperTitle: RLAnything: Forge Environment, Policy, and Reward Model in Completely Dynamic RL System
keywords: [Reinforcement Learning, Dynamic Environments, Reward Modeling, Self-Improvement, Agents]

name: rl-awb-nighttime-white-balance
description: Correct color distortion in nighttime photos by combining statistical gray-pixel detection with reinforcement learning parameter optimization. Achieves superior cross-camera generalization without extensive labeled nighttime training data through a hybrid architecture that preserves interpretability while gaining adaptive tuning capability.
paperTitle: RL-AWB: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Auto White Balance Correction in Low-Light Night-time Scenes
keywords: [Reinforcement Learning, Computational Photography, Low-Light Processing, White Balance]

name: rl-on-pretraining-data
description: Scale LLM training using RL on unlabeled pre-training corpora without human annotation. Derive reward signals directly from text segments to optimize both autoregressive generation and in-context reasoning across knowledge and mathematical domains.
paperTitle: Reinforcement Learning on Pre-Training Data
keywords: [reinforcement-learning, pre-training, llm-scaling, reward-signals, language-models]

name: rl-plus-capability-boundary
description: Hybrid-policy optimization combining multiple importance sampling and exploration-based advantage functions. Prevents capability boundary collapse while maintaining verification rewards, enabling LLMs to exceed baseline boundaries.
paperTitle: RL-PLUS - Countering Capability Boundary Collapse in RLVR
keywords: [reinforcement-learning, capability-expansion, importance-sampling, exploration]

name: rl-rubric-anchors
description: Extend RL to open-ended tasks using structured rubrics as reward anchors, enabling fine-grained evaluation of subjective outputs without requiring binary correctness signals.
paperTitle: Reinforcement Learning with Rubric Anchors
keywords: [reinforcement-learning, rubric-based-rewards, subjective-evaluation, open-ended-generation]

name: rl-verifiable-rewards
description: RLVR extends reasoning capabilities by proving answer-only rewards implicitly incentivize correct intermediate reasoning via the Logic Prior principle.
paperTitle: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards Implicitly Incentivizes Correct Reasoning
keywords: [reinforcement-learning, reasoning, verifiable-rewards, logic-prior, chain-of-thought]

name: rl-visual-reasoning
description: RL in vision-language models improves inference-time alignment between vision and reasoning in mid-to-late layers, not vision ability or reasoning separately. Strategic layer freezing enables diagnosis of which components contribute to RL gains.
paperTitle: What does RL improve for Visual Reasoning? A Frankenstein-Style Analysis
keywords: [Multimodal Learning, Reinforcement Learning, Visual Reasoning, Model Analysis, Layer Freezing]

name: rlad-abstract-discovery-reasoning
description: Train reasoning models to discover diverse solution strategies through two-player RL that jointly optimizes abstraction generation and solution derivation. Use when exploring multiple reasoning approaches is preferable to single-strategy depth.
paperTitle: RLAD: Learning to Discover Abstractions via Reasoning RL
keywords: [reasoning-abstraction, RLVR, strategy-discovery, curriculum-learning]

name: rlad-discovering-abstractions
description: Train LLMs to discover domain-specific abstractions (concise representations of procedural and factual knowledge) through two-player RL. An abstraction generator proposes key insights, a solution generator uses them to solve problems, and both receive RL rewards, learning structured reasoning that transfers to harder problems.
paperTitle: RLAD: Training LLMs to Discover Abstractions for Solving Reasoning Problems
keywords: [abstraction-learning, reasoning, RL-training, procedural-knowledge, problem-solving]

name: rlad-knowledge-distillation
description: RLAD performs selective distillation during RL, using trust-region ratio objectives to balance exploration, exploitation, and teacher guidance for efficient reasoning model training.
paperTitle: Reinforcement-aware Knowledge Distillation for LLM Reasoning
keywords: [Knowledge Distillation, Reinforcement Learning, LLM Efficiency, Student-Teacher, GRPO]

name: rlkv-cache-compression
description: Use reinforcement learning to identify reasoning-critical attention heads and apply selective KV cache compression, reducing cache by 20-50% while preserving reasoning quality. Maintains speedups up to 1.21x with minimal performance loss.
paperTitle: Which Heads Matter for Reasoning? RL-Guided KV Cache Compression
keywords: [kv-cache, compression, attention-heads, reinforcement-learning, reasoning-efficiency]

name: rlp-reinforcement-pretraining-objective
description: Improve reasoning during pretraining (not just post-training) by computing rewards from information gain—how much reasoning improves log-likelihood of observed tokens. Works at 1T token scale across diverse corpora.
paperTitle: RLP: Reinforcement as Pretraining Objective
keywords: [pretraining, RLVR, reasoning, information-gain, generalization]

name: rlp-rl-pretraining-objective
description: Integrate reinforcement learning into the pretraining phase by measuring the utility of intermediate reasoning for predicting subsequent tokens. This approach generates dense reward signals during standard pretraining, enabling models to develop reasoning abilities earlier and with significant performance gains (19% improvement on 1.7B, 45% lift on 12B models).
paperTitle: RLP: Reinforcement as a Pretraining Objective
keywords: [pretraining, RL-training, reasoning, dense-reward, efficiency]

name: rlve-scaling-rl-with-adaptive-environments
description: Scale RL training for language models by automatically adapting problem difficulty to match model capabilities using verifiable reward environments—achieving 3.37% absolute improvement on reasoning benchmarks through environment diversity rather than compute scaling alone.
paperTitle: RLVE: Scaling Up RL for LMs with Adaptive Verifiable Environments
keywords: [Reinforcement Learning, Adaptive Difficulty, Curriculum Learning, Verifiable Environments, Language Models]

name: robo-dopamine-rl
description: Overcome reward function design challenges via General Reward Model (GRM) for step-wise progress assessment. Uses multi-view observations for occlusion robustness, hop-based progress normalization, Policy-Invariant Reward Shaping—enabling 95% robot task success within 150 interactions with theoretically-grounded dense rewards.
paperTitle: Robo-Dopamine: General Process Reward Modeling for Robotic Manipulation
keywords: [robotics, reinforcement-learning, reward-modeling, multi-view, process-rewards]

name: robofactory-embodied-agent-collaboration
description: Learn coordinated manipulation behaviors for multi-robot systems using compositional constraints that enforce safe and efficient collaboration. Generate training data through automated collection with task-specific constraint interfaces, then train imitation learning policies adaptable to varying difficulty levels.
paperTitle: RoboFactory: Exploring Embodied Agent Collaboration with Compositional Constraints
keywords: [Multi-Robot Systems, Embodied AI, Imitation Learning, Compositional Constraints, Manipulation Tasks]

name: robomemory-multi-memory-embodied-ai
description: Brain-inspired framework integrating spatial, temporal, episodic, and semantic memory systems for embodied agents. Achieves 26.5% performance improvement via dynamic spatial knowledge graphs and closed-loop planning with critic modules.
paperTitle: RoboMemory Brain-Inspired Multi-Memory Agentic Framework
keywords: [embodied-ai, memory-systems, robotic-agents, knowledge-graphs, long-horizon-planning]

name: roboscape-physics-world-model
description: Generate physically plausible robot manipulation videos by jointly learning RGB generation, temporal depth prediction, and keypoint dynamics. Enables training manipulation policies on synthetic data with strong correlation to simulator performance.
paperTitle: RoboScape: Physics-informed Embodied World Model
keywords: [World Models, Robotic Vision, Physics-aware Video Generation, Keypoint Tracking, Embodied AI]

name: robot-r1-embodied-reasoning
description: Train vision-language models for robotic manipulation using RL to improve embodied reasoning about spatial relationships and movements, achieving 31% improvement on manipulation benchmarks.
paperTitle: Robot-R1: Reinforcement Learning for Enhanced Embodied Reasoning in Robotics
keywords: [robotics, reinforcement-learning, embodied-reasoning, vision-language-models, spatial-reasoning]

name: robovip-robot-video-synthesis
description: Generate synthetic robot manipulation data via diffusion models using visual identity prompting from exemplar images. Improve multi-view temporal coherence and scalability for robot policy training without extensive real-world data collection.
paperTitle: RoboVIP: Multi-View Video Generation with Visual Identity Prompting Augments Robot Manipulation
keywords: ['Robot Learning', 'Video Generation', 'Data Augmentation']

name: robust-r1
description: Enable MLLMs to handle visually degraded images by explicitly reasoning about degradation types and severity. Trains models to perceive degradation parameters, analyze semantic impact, and reconstruct interpretations while adapting reasoning depth to degradation complexity—achieving robust understanding with interpretable explanations.
paperTitle: Robust-R1: Degradation-Aware Reasoning for Robust Visual Understanding
keywords: [vision, degradation, reasoning, robustness, multimodal, llm]

name: rocket-model-compression
description: Compress language models by formulating heterogeneous layer-wise allocation as a constrained knapsack problem. Measure per-layer compression error, solve via dynamic programming to minimize total error within target model size, and avoid pathological solutions where some layers are severely damaged.
paperTitle: ROCKET: Rapid Optimization via Calibration-guided Knapsack for Model Compression
keywords: [Model Compression, Pruning, Quantization, Knapsack Optimization, Layer-wise Allocation]

name: roi-reasoning-rational-optimization-for-inference
description: Advanced reasoning approach for optimizing inference efficiency through meta-cognitive planning, enabling agents to make better decisions with reduced computational overhead.
paperTitle: ROI-Reasoning: Rational Optimization for Inference via Pre-Computation Meta-Cognition
keywords: ['reasoning']

name: rstar2-agent-reasoning
description: Train efficient 14B-parameter agents via GRPO with resample-on-correct rollout strategy and Python code execution, achieving state-of-the-art reasoning through autonomous exploration and iterative refinement
paperTitle: rStar2-Agent Agentic Reasoning
keywords: [agentic-rl, code-execution, reinforcement-learning, python, grpo]

name: ruscarl-rubric-scaffolded-rl
description: Guide LLM exploration through rubric-based scaffolding that gradually diminishes, enabling models to internalize reasoning patterns while maintaining exploration quality for robust RL training.
paperTitle: RuscaRL: Rubric-Scaffolded RL for General LLM Reasoning
keywords: [reinforcement-learning, rubric-guidance, exploration-guidance, reasoning-scaffolding, curriculum-learning]

name: rwml-reinforcement-world-models
description: Train LLM agents to anticipate environment consequences by learning world models through reinforcement learning with embedding-space similarity rewards, avoiding task-specific labels while enabling robust environment adaptation.
paperTitle: Reinforcement World Model Learning for LLM-based Agents
keywords: [World Models, Self-Supervised Learning, GRPO, Agent Adaptation, Embedding Space]

name: safety-at-one-shot-lm-repair
description: Recover safety alignment in fine-tuned LLMs using only a single safety example, without sacrificing utility. Leverages low-rank structure of safety gradients to enable minimal-cost correction even when models have been compromised by extensive harmful training data.
paperTitle: Safety at One Shot: Patching Fine-Tuned LLMs with A Single Instance
keywords: [LLM Safety, Model Alignment, Efficient Fine-Tuning, Low-Rank Adaptation]

name: saffron-safety-scaling
description: Implement Saffron's multifurcation reward model approach to achieve efficient inference-time safety scaling, improving robustness against prompt injection attacks while reducing computational overhead.
paperTitle: Saffron-1: Inference Scaling Paradigm for LLM Safety Assurance
keywords: [llm-safety, inference-scaling, reward-models, tree-search, adversarial-robustness]

name: sage-long-video-agents
description: Enable agents to reason over long videos through multi-turn reasoning for complex questions and single-turn answering for simpler queries. Equip orchestrator VLM with multiple tools (web search, speech transcription, temporal grounding). Use synthetic data generation and multi-reward RL achieving 6.1% improvements on open-ended tasks.
paperTitle: SAGE: Smart Any-Horizon Agents with Reinforcement Learning for Long-Context Video Reasoning
keywords: [video-understanding, agent-reasoning, reinforcement-learning, long-context, multi-turn-reasoning]

name: sage-retrieval-research-agents
description: Build effective retrieval systems for deep research agents by understanding query-retriever mismatch, preferring BM25 for keyword-oriented queries, and augmenting corpus documents with metadata and LLM-generated keywords to improve information discovery.
paperTitle: SAGE: Benchmarking and Improving Retrieval for Deep Research Agents
keywords: [Retrieval Systems, Research Agents, Query Decomposition, Corpus Augmentation, Information Seeking]

name: sage-rl-agent
description: Enable agents to continuously improve by accumulating reusable skills across sequential task chains. Train via GRPO across task sequences where skills persist and compound, provide dual rewards for both task completion and skill generation/reuse—improving completion rates 8.9% while reducing token costs by 59% compared to non-skill baselines.
paperTitle: RL for Self-Improving Agent with Skill Library
keywords: [reinforcement-learning, agents, skill-learning, self-improvement, grpo]

name: sage-self-hinting-rl
description: Inject privileged hints during GRPO training to reshape rollout distributions when advantage collapses occur, increasing outcome diversity without changing task rewards. Hints removed at deployment; policy automatically learns when to use hints via online refresh mechanism.
paperTitle: Self-Hinting Language Models Enhance Reinforcement Learning
keywords: [Reinforcement Learning, Training Instability, Reward Shaping, Curriculum Learning, GRPO]

name: sail-rl-adaptive-reasoning
description: Teach multimodal models to determine when deep reasoning is necessary and how to reason effectively through dual-reward reinforcement learning, preventing both overthinking on simple tasks and underthinking on complex ones while reducing hallucinations.
paperTitle: SAIL-RL: Guiding MLLMs in When and How to Think via Dual-Reward RL
keywords: [Multimodal RL, Thinking Control, Adaptive Reasoning, Reward Shaping, Post-Training]

name: salad-achieve-high-sparsity-attention-via-efficien
description: Implement techniques from SALAD: Achieve High-Sparsity Attention via Efficient Linear Attention Tuning for Video Diffusion Transformer. Diffusion Transformers have recently demonstrated remarkable performance in video generation
paperTitle: SALAD: Achieve High-Sparsity Attention via Efficient Linear Attention Tuning for Video Diffusion Transformer
keywords: ["research", "methodology"]

name: sample-more-think-less-gfpo
description: Group Filtered Policy Optimization (GFPO) reduces inference-time computation by sampling larger groups during training and filtering responses based on length and token efficiency to teach models efficient reasoning.

name: samtok-mask-tokenization
description: Tokenize any region mask into two special tokens, enabling pixel-wise tasks like segmentation and region understanding in multimodal language models without architectural changes. Use when adapting vision-language models to perform pixel-level understanding and manipulation.
paperTitle: SAMTok: Representing Any Mask with Two Words
keywords: [mask-tokenization, multimodal-models, segmentation, discrete-representation, pixel-tasks]

name: scale-selective-test-time
description: Decomposes math problems into sequential sub-problems, assesses difficulty, and allocates simple ones to fast System 1 reasoning while directing complex ones to deliberate System 2. Save 33-53% tokens while improving accuracy by up to 13.75 points on AIME.
